title: The Belmont Jewelry Heist
summary: On the night of March 15, 2024, Belmont Fine Jewelers on Rue de Rivoli was burglarized. Approximately 2.3 million euros in diamonds and watches were stolen. The alarm system was disabled at 2:47 AM using an insider code. Security footage shows a single masked figure entering through the back door. The lock showed no signs of forced entry. An anonymous tip led police to Marc Duval, a former employee fired 3 months prior.
key_facts: Break-in occurred between 2:30 AM and 3:15 AM on March 15, 2024
Alarm was disabled using an insider code
No forced entry — lock was picked or opened with a key
Marc Duval was terminated on December 12, 2023 for insubordination
Duval had access to alarm codes during his employment
A white van was seen near the store at 2:20 AM
Stolen goods: 47 diamonds, 12 luxury watches, total value 2.3M euros
Duval's fingerprint was found on the back door handle
Duval claims he hasn't been near the store since December
Cell tower data places Duval's phone in the area between 1:00 AM and 4:00 AM
evidence: Security footage (masked figure, no face visible)
Disabled alarm log showing insider code used
Fingerprint match on back door handle
Cell tower data placing Duval's phone in the neighborhood
